name: Nuitka (Python-to-C Compiler for Executable Distribution)
pricing_notes: Free (Apache-2.0) for basic use; Nuitka Commercial for enterprise features (code obfuscation, onefile acceleration)

auth.notes: CLI tool; commercial license via nuitka.net for commercial features

performance.notes: Compiled executables run 10-30% faster than CPython; build time is long (minutes)

agent_readiness.notes: Python-to-C compiler that produces native executables. python -m nuitka --standalone script.py. python -m nuitka --onefile script.py. python -m nuitka --module mypackage/ (Python extension). --follow-imports includes all imports. --plugin-enable=tk-inter for Tkinter. --plugin-enable=numpy,torch for ML. Compilation: translates AST to C, then compiles with GCC/MSVC/clang. Output: dist/script.exe or script.dist/. Obfuscation: commercial feature. Faster startup than PyInstaller --onefile. Supports most Python code including ctypes and cffi.

gotchas: Build time is very long (5-30 minutes for large projects)
Some Python features compile poorly (exec/eval, dynamic imports)
Must build on target OS — no cross-compilation
Plugin system required for specific frameworks (Qt, Tk, numpy, torch)
best_when: When code protection/obfuscation is needed (commercial license)
Performance improvement over PyInstaller for startup time
Distributing ML apps that need compiled performance
Creating Python extension modules (.so/.pyd) from Python code
avoid_when: Dynamic Python features heavily used (exec/eval/getattr-heavy code)
When obfuscation is the only goal but cost is a barrier
summary: Nuitka compiles Python to C, producing native executables 10-30% faster than CPython. python -m nuitka --standalone script.py. --onefile for single binary. Plugins for Qt, Tk, numpy, torch. Code obfuscation requires commercial license. Much longer build time than PyInstaller. Must build on target OS. Apache-2.0 for basic use; commercial for obfuscation. Best when performance or IP protection justifies the complexity over PyInstaller.
